contest.title: 2025-2026 ICPC Asia Taichung Regional Contest (Unrated, Online Mirror, ICPC Rules, Teams Preferred)

sources.notes: Bus-walking optimization with faster buses and ordered intervals.
Independent writeup changes reference frame and describes repeatedly taking the next reachable bus segment.

annotation.summary: Minimize arrival time when a person can walk and board faster buses moving toward the destination.
annotation.constraints: The number of buses is large enough that simulating every possible transfer naively is not intended.
annotation.core_idea: Switch to a reference frame where buses become horizontal segments and the walking trajectory is a line. The optimal strategy is greedy: keep moving toward the destination and board the reachable bus segment that advances the trajectory earliest/farthest, then continue from the bus endpoint. Sorting/querying candidate segments by the derived line-intersection inequality supports this process.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) with sorting and ordered candidate queries.
tricks: greedily board the next useful bus

solution_variants.name: time-space-greedy
solution_variants.summary: Represent walking and buses in an s-t diagram and greedily jump to the next intersecting bus segment.

tags.evidence: The algorithm repeatedly takes the next useful bus once it becomes reachable.
Buses must be ordered/indexed to find the next intersecting segment efficiently.
The core interpretation uses line and segment intersections in a transformed coordinate system.
